tracks.meaning: a sudden loss of brain function caused by interruption of blood flow to the brain, often resulting in paralysis or loss of consciousness
the act of moving an oar through water to propel a boat or shell forward in rowing
a single movement of a tool, pen, or brush across a surface
a particular style or method of swimming such as freestyle, backstroke, or butterfly
a bundle of nerve fibers in the spinal cord or brain that carries signals in a particular direction
the act of moving something with a steady continuous motion
a line or mark made by drawing a pen, pencil, or brush across a surface
a movement of a watch or clock hand indicating the passage of time
a clever or effective action or move in a situation
the motion of a conductor's baton upward or downward marking a beat
a single transaction or business dealing
a mental or emotional occurrence such as a flash of genius or moment of understanding
